# Beudox HR Management System - Technical Context

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a comprehensive React TypeScript application built with Vite, using Supabase as the backend. The system implements role-based access control and manages complex HR workflows including employee management, payroll, evaluations, and project tracking.

## Technology Stack

### Frontend Framework
- React 18 with TypeScript
- Vite build tool with SWC for fast compilation
- React Router v6 for client-side routing
- TanStack Query for server state management and caching

### UI & Styling
- Tailwind CSS for utility-first styling
- Radix UI primitives via shadcn/ui component library
- Lucide React for consistent iconography
- Custom CSS variables for theming

### Forms & Validation
- React Hook Form for performant form handling
- Zod for runtime type validation and schema definition
- @hookform/resolvers for integration

### Rich Content
- Tiptap editor for rich text editing in HR policies
- Support for headings, lists, links, and formatting

### Data Visualization
- Recharts for charts and graphs in financial reports
- React Day Picker for date selection components

### File Processing
- XLSX library for Excel file import/export
- React Image Crop for avatar management

### Development Tools
- Vitest for unit testing with jsdom
- Playwright for end-to-end testing
- ESLint with TypeScript rules
- PostCSS with Autoprefixer

## Application Structure

### Routing & Navigation
The app uses protected routes with role-based access control. Key routes include:
- Authentication flows (/login, /set-password)
- Dashboard (/dashboard)
- Employee management (/employees/*)
- Project management (/projects/*)
- Financial modules (/payroll, /invoices, /finance)
- HR operations (/leave, /evaluations, /hr-policies)
- Settings (/settings)

### Component Architecture
- Layout components (AppLayout, AppSidebar, TopBar)
- Page components for each route
- Reusable UI components from shadcn/ui
- Business-specific components (EvaluationTimeline, RichTextEditor)
- Form components with validation

### State Management
- React Query for server state (API data)
- React Context for authentication state
- Local component state for UI interactions
- URL state for routing and filters

## Database & Backend

### Supabase Integration
- Authentication with email/password and invite flows
- PostgreSQL database with Row Level Security
- Real-time subscriptions for live updates
- Storage for file uploads (avatars, documents)
- Edge Functions for server-side processing (payroll generation, email sending)

### Key Database Tables
- employees: Core employee data with role assignments
- companies: Multi-tenant company information
- evaluations: Quarterly performance reviews
- daily_evaluations: Daily feedback system
- leave_requests: Leave management with balances
- payroll: Payroll processing and payslips
- projects: Project management with assignments
- invoices: Client invoicing system
- hr_policies: Rich text policy documents
- loans: Employee loan tracking

## Business Logic

### Role-Based Access Control
Implemented through:
- canAccess utility function checking user roles against route permissions
- Database RLS policies for data-level security
- UI-level hiding/disabling based on permissions
- Role hierarchy: CEO > HR Manager > Team Lead > Employee

### Key Business Rules
- Leave balance calculations with carry-over logic
- Payroll generation with overtime and allowance calculations
- Evaluation workflows with visibility restrictions
- Notification triggers for key events
- Invoice generation with PDF creation

### Data Flow Patterns
- React Query for optimistic updates and caching
- Form submissions with validation and error handling
- Real-time updates for collaborative features
- Background processing via Supabase functions

## Development Patterns

### Code Organization
- Feature-based folder structure under src/
- Shared utilities in lib/ (utils, formatters, validations)
- Type definitions from Supabase generated types
- Component composition with custom hooks

### Testing Strategy
- Unit tests for utilities and hooks
- Integration tests for components
- E2E tests for critical user flows
- Playwright for browser automation

### Performance Considerations
- Code splitting with dynamic imports
- Image optimization and lazy loading
- Query optimization with React Query
- Bundle analysis and tree shaking

## Deployment & DevOps

### Build Process
- Vite production build with asset optimization
- Environment-specific configurations
- Static asset handling

### Development Workflow
- Hot module replacement during development
- Component tagging for development insights
- Linting and type checking
- Automated testing pipelines

This technical context provides the foundation for understanding the Beudox HR codebase and making informed decisions about modifications and extensions.
